subject: Chemistry
options: Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect

Both the statements I and II are correct
solution:
Drinking water has a BOD level of 1 - 2 ppm. When the BOD value of water is in the range 3 - 5 ppm, the water is moderately clean. Polluted water has a BOD value in the range of 6 - 9 ppm. Healthy water should generally have dissolved oxygen concentrations above 6.5-8 mg/L

Drinking water with concentrations of nitrate (measured as nitrate-nitrogen) below 10 mg of nitrate per liter of water (mg/L) is considered safe. Permissible limit of Zn in potable water is 5 ppm.

subject: Chemistry
options: Polluted water has BOD value higher than $10 \mathrm{ppm}$
Aerobic bacteria decreases the BOD value
Anaerobic bacteria increases the BOD value
Clean water has BOD value higher than 10 ppm
solution: Clean water has BOD value less than $5 \mathrm{ppm}$. Polluted water has BOD value higher than 10 ppm.

in the atmosphere, it is depleted by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CFC</mi></math>s.
in the stratosphere, it forms a protective shield against UV radiation.

It is a toxic gas and its reaction with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NO</mi></math> gives <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>

CF2Cl2g⟶UVC˙lg+C˙F2Clg

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Chlorofluorocarbon compounds (CFCs) are non-reactive, non-flammable molecules that are used in refrigerators, air conditioners, plastics, and electronic industries. Once released CFCs mix with atmospheric gases and reach the stratosphere, where they are decomposed by UV radiations.

CF2Cl2g→uvCl⊙g+C⊙F2Clg

The chlorine radical released will react with ozone.

Cl⊙g+O3g→O2g+ClOg⊙

The Cl⊙O will generate chlorine radicals again

ClOg⊙+Og→O2g+Cl⊙

subject: Chemistry
options: protects us from the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math>-ray radiation
solution:
The presence of ozone in troposphere generates photochemical smog.

Ozone acts as an important infrared absorber (greenhouse gas), particularly in the upper troposphere, and also is an absorber of solar ultraviolet (UV) radiation.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Photochemical smog is formed on the chemical reaction of sunlight, nitrogen oxides and volatile organic compounds in the atmosphere.

NO2g→hvNOg+Og

Oxygen atom (nascent) is very reactive and combines with O2 in air to produce ozone.

Og+O2g→O3g

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Species Maximum allowed concentration in ppm in drinking water
A. F- 2
B. SO42- 500
C. NO3- 50
D. Zn  5

F– ion concentration above 2 ppm causes brown mottling of teeth. At the same time, excess fluoride (over 10 ppm) causes harmful effect to bones and teeth.

Excessive sulphate (>500 ppm) in drinking water causes laxative effect, otherwise at moderate levels it is harmless.

The maximum limit of nitrate in drinking water is 50 ppm. Excess nitrate in drinking water can cause disease such as methemoglobinemia (‘blue baby’ syndrome).

subject: Chemistry
solution: Wet deposition is the acidic water received through rain, fog, snow, dew, mist, etc. Dry deposition is deposition of wind blown acidic gases and particles on the ground. Ammonium salt in rain drop result in wet deposition. Oxides of Nitrogen and sulphur settle down on ground as dry deposition.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Dichlorodiphenyltrichloroethane (DDT) is an insecticide used in agriculture. Dieldrin a decades-old chlorocarbon insecticide that has long been banned from use in most of the world. It was introduced in 1948 by the now-defunct J. Organophosphates are a group of human-made chemicals that poison insects and mammals. Organophosphates are the most widely used insecticides today. They are used in agriculture, the home, gardens, and veterinary practice.

These days, the pesticide industry has shifted its attention to herbicides such as sodium chlorate (NaClO3), sodium arsenite (Na3AsO3 ) and many others.

subject: Chemistry
options: Both Assertion and Reason are true and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are true but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion.
Assertion is true but Reason is false.
Assertion is false but Reason is true.
solution: The amount of oxygen required by bacteria to break down the organic matter present in a certain volume of a sample of water, is called Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD). The amount of BOD in the water is a measure of the amount of organic material in the water, in terms of how much oxygen will be required to break it down biologically. Clean water would have BOD value of less than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>ppm</mi></math> whereas highly polluted water could have a BOD value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>17</mn><mo> </mo><mi>ppm</mi></math> or more.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Formula of ppm is-

ppm=weight of soluteweight of solution×106

ppm=10.3×10-31030×106 =10

PPM is a concentration term and its full form is "parts per million" and it also can be expressed as milligrams per liter (mg/L). This measurement is the mass of a chemical or contaminate per unit volume of water.

subject: Chemistry
solution: On heating concentration of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> in water decreases. So boiling water and water at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>80</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> having less <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> concentration. Polluted water also having less <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> concentration. So water at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> is having maximum <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> concentration.

solution: Carbon monoxide combines with haemoglobin to form carboxyhaemoglobin. High concentration of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo> </mo></math>leads to stiffness of flower buds.  Polychlorinated biphenyls, are highly toxic industrial compounds. Pyrus and Vitis can metabolise nitrogen oxide.

subject: Chemistry
solution: Methane leads to both global warming & photochemical smog. Methane is generated in large amounts from paddy fields. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> can be absorbed by photosynthesis, or by formation of acid rain etc., while no such activities are there for methane. Hence methane is stronger global warming gas than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>, Methane is not a component of reducing smog.

solution:
Clean water would have BOD value of less than 5 ppm whereas highly polluted water could have a BOD value of 17 ppm or more.

For survival of aquatic life dissolved oxygen is responsible its optimum limit 6.5 ppm and optimum limit of BOD ranges from 10-20 ppm & BOD stands for biochemical oxygen demand.

subject: Chemistry

solution:
The amount of BOD in the water is a measure of the amount of organic material in the water, in terms of how much oxygen will be required to break it down biologically.

Clean water has BOD value of less than 5 ppm whereas polluted water has a BOD value of 17 ppm or more.

So, the pond has very clean water.

subject: Chemistry
options: Increase in amount of dissolved oxygen in water
solution: Addition of phosphate fertilizers or other fertilizers to water bodies causes enrichment in nutrients, this phenomenon is called Eutrophication. Increase in nutrients enhanced growth of algae (vegetation) which cause consume of dissolved oxygen. Hence decrease in quantity of dissolved oxygen, effects the life of aqua-animals or other vegetation. So Eutrophication is a types of water pollution.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Herbicides, also commonly known as weedkillers, are chemical substances used to control unwanted plants. Selective herbicides control specific weed species, while leaving the desired crop relatively unharmed, while non-selective herbicides can be used to clear waste ground, industrial and construction sites, railways and railway embankments as they kill all plant material with which they come into contact.

Nowadays, the pesticide industry has shifted its attention to herbicides such as sodium chlorate NaClO3, sodium arsinite Na3AsO3 and many others.

subject: Chemistry
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
solution:
Since eutrophication is result of excessive growth of weed in water bodies, which consume dissolved oxygen of water bodies. So, Eutrophication decreases amount of dissolved oxygen in water bodies.

Polluted water has low value of dissolved oxygen, but high value of BOD (Biological oxygen demand), since more organic matter requires more dissolved oxygen to get decompose. Polluted water has BOD grater than 17.

subject: Chemistry
solution: CO2 is used in dry cleaning is a better strategy to control environmental pollution. Liquid carbon dioxide is better to replace conventional halogenated solvents which are potentially carcinogenic. These detergents are developed in a way that one end of the molecule is soluble in non-polar substances like grease, oil stains and the other end dissolves in liquid carbon dioxide.

subject: Chemistry
solution: About <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>75</mn><mo>%</mo></math> of the solar energy reaching the earth is absorbed by the earth’s surface, which increases its temperature. The rest of the heat radiates back to the atmosphere. Some of the heat is trapped by gases such as carbon dioxide, methane, ozone, chlorofluorocarbon compounds (CFCs) and water vapour in the atmosphere. Thus, they add to the heating of the atmosphere. This causes global warming. Hence, Nitrogen gas is not responsible for green house effect.
